title: aquilegia vulgaris subsp. aggericola
intro: Aquilegia vulgaris subsp. aggericola, commonly known as the alpine columbine or rocky alpine meadow columbine, is a subspecies of the common columbine plant native to the mountainous regions of Europe. This perennial flowering plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic properties. The health benefits of Aquilegia vulgaris subsp. aggericola include relief from urinary tract issues, such as kidney stones and infections, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with respiratory conditions. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it for medicinal purposes.
